content: The 2024-2025 wave of state-of-the-art active learning agent memory architectures can leverage intentional, hard working memory capacity limits to drive more efficient curiosity-driven exploration, rather than pursuing unbounded memory scaling that creates redundant information overload. This 'bounded active curiosity' framework forces agents to proactively query for only high-value missing information that fills gaps in their limited working memory, rather than passively storing every available observation.
source_topics: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
